seo.title: Rhode Island Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Rhode Island — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license rhode island, dental assistant certification rhode island, RDA rhode island, DANB CDA rhode island, dental assistant radiography rhode island

board.name: Rhode Island Board of Examiners in Dentistry
board.mailing_address: Rhode Island Department of Health, Board of Examiners in Dentistry, 3 Capitol Hill, Room 104, Providence, RI 02908-5097

license_types.title: Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Rhode Island.

steps.title: Create an RIHPOS Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Rhode Island Health Professionals Online System (RIHPOS) portal and register as a new user to create your professional licensing account.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Log into your RIHPOS account, select 'Start a New Application', and choose 'Registered Dental Assistant' as your license type.

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application form within RIHPOS, providing personal, educational, and professional history information.

steps.title: Upload Required Documentation
steps.description: Electronically submit copies of your high school diploma or GED, proof of DANB CDA certification or CODA-accredited program completion plus an approved exam, and current CPR or BLS certification through the RIHPOS system.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable initial application fee of $50 using the online payment system available in RIHPOS.

steps.title: Submit and Monitor Application
steps.description: Review your complete application, submit it electronically via RIHPOS, and regularly monitor its status through your RIHPOS dashboard for updates or requests for additional information.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the Rhode Island Board of Examiners in Dentistry by registration.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rhode Island requires Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Rhode Island.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $25. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Assistant through the Rhode Island Board of Examiners in Dentistry by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Rhode Island
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant license in Rhode Island, use the Rhode Island Health Professionals Online System (RIHPOS) portal for all application submissions.

official_links.portal_name: Rhode Island Health Professionals Online System (RIHPOS)
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the RIHPOS portal, click 'Register a Person' to create a new account, and follow the prompts to set up your login credentials before starting an application.
